title: My Father's Walking Speed
paragraphs: Last Sunday, I walked with my father.
When I was small, he was always too fast.
I had to run to follow him.
Sunday, I noticed something.
He was walking slower than me.
I had to slow down. I had to wait.
He did not look at me. He just walked.
But I knew. Time turned around.
translation: 지난주 일요일, 아버지와 같이 걸었어요.
어렸을 때, 아버지는 늘 너무 빨랐어요.
따라가려고 뛰어야 했어요.
일요일에, 뭔가를 알아챘어요.
아버지가 저보다 천천히 걷고 있었어요.
제가 느려져야 했어요. 기다려야 했어요.
아버지는 저를 안 봤어요. 그냥 걸었어요.
그런데 알았어요. 시간이 돌았어요.

quiz.question: When the writer was small, how did her father walk?
What did the writer realize on Sunday?
options: Time turned around — she had to slow down for him

title: My Father's Walking Speed
paragraphs: Last Sunday afternoon, I went for a walk in the park with my old father.
When I was a small child, he was always walking too fast for me.
I had to half-run, half-walk, just to follow his strong steps.
But last Sunday, somewhere in the park, I noticed something quiet.
He was walking much more slowly than I was now.
I had to slow my own steps. I had to wait gently for him.
He did not look up at me. He just walked at his new speed.
But I knew without words. Time had turned around without asking us.
translation: 지난주 일요일 오후, 늙으신 아버지와 공원에서 산책을 갔어요.
어렸을 때, 아버지는 늘 저에겐 너무 빨랐어요.
그 단단한 걸음을 따라가려고 거의 뛰어야 했어요.
그런데 지난 일요일에, 공원 어딘가에서, 조용한 뭔가를 알아챘어요.
아버지가 저보다 훨씬 천천히 걷고 있었어요.
제가 제 걸음을 늦춰야 했어요. 부드럽게 기다려야 했어요.
아버지는 저를 올려다보지 않았어요. 그냥 자기 새 속도로 걸었어요.
그런데 말 없이 알았어요. 시간이 우리에게 묻지도 않고 돌았어요.

quiz.question: How did the writer have to walk as a child?
quiz.questionKo: 어릴 때 글쓴이는 어떻게 걸어야 했나요?
options: Half-run, half-walk to follow her father

quiz.question: What did the writer know without words?
options: Time had turned around without asking them

title: My Father's Walking Speed
paragraphs: Last Sunday afternoon, I went for a slow walk in the neighborhood park with my aging father.
When I was a small child years ago, he was always walking too fast for me, his long stride impossible to match.
I had to half-run, half-walk, almost embarrassed, just to follow his strong purposeful steps down the sidewalk.
But last Sunday, somewhere along the wide central path of the park, I noticed something quiet and important.
He was now walking much more slowly than I was, and it was not because he was thinking.
I had to slow my own steps without comment. I had to wait gently and patiently for him.
He did not look up at me or seem to notice. He just walked at his new, careful speed.
But I knew without any words. Time had quietly turned around inside our small family, without ever asking us.
translation: 지난주 일요일 오후, 늙어가시는 아버지와 동네 공원에서 천천히 산책을 갔어요.
몇 년 전 어렸을 때, 아버지는 늘 저에겐 너무 빨랐어요. 그 긴 보폭은 따라잡을 수 없었어요.
거의 부끄러워하며, 강하고 목적 있는 그 걸음을 따라가려고 인도에서 반은 뛰고 반은 걸어야 했어요.
그런데 지난 일요일에, 공원의 넓은 중앙 길 어딘가에서, 조용하고 중요한 뭔가를 알아챘어요.
아버지가 지금은 저보다 훨씬 천천히 걷고 있었어요. 그리고 그건 생각 중이라서가 아니었어요.
코멘트 없이 제 걸음을 늦춰야 했어요. 부드럽게 그리고 끈기 있게 기다려야 했어요.
아버지는 저를 올려다보거나 알아채지 않았어요. 그냥 새로운, 조심스러운 속도로 걸었어요.
그런데 말 한 마디 없이 알았어요. 시간이 우리 작은 가족 안에서, 우리에게 묻지도 않고, 조용히 돌아 있었어요.

quiz.question: Why was the father walking slower last Sunday?
quiz.questionKo: 지난 일요일 아버지가 더 천천히 걸은 이유는?
options: Not because he was thinking — it was age

quiz.question: What turned around inside the small family?

title: My Father's Walking Speed
paragraphs: Last Sunday afternoon, I went for a slow deliberate walk in the neighborhood park with my visibly aging father.
When I was a small child years and years ago, he was always walking too fast for me, his long stride impossible for my short legs to comprehensively match.
I had to half-run, half-walk, almost embarrassed by my own slowness, just to follow his strong purposeful steps down the sidewalk.
But last Sunday, somewhere along the wide central path of the park, I quietly noticed something small and unexpectedly important.
He was now walking much more slowly than I was, without any announcement, and it was not because he was thinking about something difficult.
I had to slow my own steps without any verbal comment whatsoever. I had to wait gently and patiently for him, without making it visible.
He did not look up at me or seem to notice the careful adjustment. He just walked at his new, careful, smaller speed.
But I knew without any words. Time had quietly turned around inside our small family, without ever asking either of us for our permission first.
translation: 지난주 일요일 오후, 눈에 띄게 늙어가시는 아버지와 동네 공원에서 천천히 의도적으로 산책을 갔어요.
몇 년 전 어렸을 때, 아버지는 늘 저에겐 너무 빨랐어요. 그 긴 보폭은 제 짧은 다리로 포괄적으로 따라잡을 수 없었어요.
제 자신의 느림에 거의 부끄러워하며, 강하고 목적 있는 그 걸음을 따라가려고 인도에서 반은 뛰고 반은 걸어야 했어요.
그런데 지난 일요일에, 공원의 넓은 중앙 길 어딘가에서, 조용히 작고 예상치 못하게 중요한 뭔가를 알아챘어요.
아버지가 지금은 저보다 훨씬 천천히, 어떤 알림도 없이 걷고 있었어요. 그리고 그건 어려운 무엇을 생각 중이라서가 아니었어요.
어떤 언어적 코멘트도 없이 제 걸음을 늦춰야 했어요. 부드럽게 그리고 끈기 있게, 보이지 않게 기다려야 했어요.
아버지는 저를 올려다보거나 그 신경 쓴 조정을 알아채지 않았어요. 그냥 새로운, 조심스러운, 더 작아진 속도로 걸었어요.
그런데 말 한 마디 없이 알았어요. 시간이 우리 작은 가족 안에서, 우리 둘 누구의 허락도 먼저 묻지 않고, 조용히 돌아 있었어요.

quiz.question: Why did the writer slow her own steps?
options: To wait gently and patiently for him, without making it visible
She wanted to admire the park

quiz.question: What had time done without anyone's permission?
quiz.questionKo: 시간은 누구의 허락도 없이 무엇을 했나요?
options: Quietly turned around inside their small family

title: My Father's Walking Speed
paragraphs: Last Sunday afternoon, I went for a slow deliberate walk in the comprehensive neighborhood park with my visibly aging father.
When I was a small fundamental child years and years ago, he was always walking too fast for me, his long stride impossible for my short legs to comprehensively match.
I had to half-run, half-walk, almost fundamentally embarrassed by my own slowness, just to follow his strong purposeful steps down the long sidewalk.
But last Sunday, somewhere along the wide elaborate central path of the park, I quietly noticed something small and unexpectedly fundamental.
He was now walking much more slowly than I was, without any announcement or comprehensive acknowledgment, and it was not because he was thinking about something difficult.
I had to slow my own steps without any verbal comment whatsoever. I had to wait gently and elaborately and patiently for him, without making the adjustment visible.
He did not look up at me or seem to fundamentally notice the careful adjustment. He just walked at his new, careful, smaller, comprehensive speed.
But I knew without any words. Time had quietly and fundamentally turned around inside our small family, without ever asking either of us for our preliminary permission.
translation: 지난주 일요일 오후, 눈에 띄게 늙어가시는 아버지와 포괄적인 동네 공원에서 천천히 의도적으로 산책을 갔어요.
몇 년 전, 근본적으로 어린 아이였을 때, 아버지는 늘 저에겐 너무 빨랐어요. 그 긴 보폭은 제 짧은 다리로 포괄적으로 따라잡을 수 없었어요.
제 자신의 느림에 거의 근본적으로 부끄러워하며, 강하고 목적 있는 그 걸음을 따라가려고 긴 인도에서 반은 뛰고 반은 걸어야 했어요.
그런데 지난 일요일에, 공원의 넓고 정교한 중앙 길 어딘가에서, 조용히 작고 예상치 못하게 근본적인 뭔가를 알아챘어요.
아버지가 지금은 저보다 훨씬 천천히, 어떤 알림이나 포괄적인 인정도 없이 걷고 있었어요. 그리고 그건 어려운 무엇을 생각 중이라서가 아니었어요.
어떤 언어적 코멘트도 없이 제 걸음을 늦춰야 했어요. 부드럽게 그리고 정교하게 그리고 끈기 있게 기다려야 했어요. 그 조정이 보이지 않게요.
아버지는 저를 올려다보거나 그 신경 쓴 조정을 근본적으로 알아채지 않았어요. 그냥 새로운, 조심스러운, 더 작아진, 포괄적인 속도로 걸었어요.
그런데 말 한 마디 없이 알았어요. 시간이 우리 작은 가족 안에서, 우리 둘 누구의 예비적 허락도 먼저 묻지 않고, 조용히 그리고 근본적으로 돌아 있었어요.

quiz.question: How does the writer describe the father's new walking speed?
quiz.questionKo: 글쓴이는 아버지의 새 걸음 속도를 어떻게 묘사하나요?
options: His new, careful, smaller, comprehensive speed

quiz.question: What did time do fundamentally?
options: Quietly and fundamentally turned around inside their small family

title: My Father's Walking Speed
paragraphs: Last Sunday afternoon, I went for a slow deliberate walk in the comprehensive neighborhood park with my visibly aging father, the air carrying the unprecedented quiet of late spring.
When I was a small fundamental child years and years ago, he was always walking too fast for me, his long unhesitating stride impossible for my short legs to comprehensively match.
I had to half-run, half-walk, almost fundamentally embarrassed by my own structural slowness, just to follow his strong purposeful steps down the long sidewalk past the houses.
But last Sunday, somewhere along the wide elaborate central path of the park beneath the tall trees, I quietly noticed something small and unexpectedly fundamental, paradigm-altering.
He was now walking much more slowly than I was, without any announcement or comprehensive acknowledgment, and it was not because he was thinking deliberately about anything difficult.
I had to slow my own steps without any verbal comment whatsoever, without any visible juxtapose between my pace and his. I had to wait gently and elaborately and patiently for him.
He did not look up at me or seem to fundamentally notice the careful internal adjustment. He just walked at his new, careful, smaller, comprehensive speed, his eyes on the path.
But I knew without any words. Time had quietly and fundamentally turned around inside our small family, in the unprecedented way it eventually does, without ever asking either of us for permission.
translation: 지난주 일요일 오후, 눈에 띄게 늙어가시는 아버지와 포괄적인 동네 공원에서 천천히 의도적으로 산책을 갔어요. 공기에 늦봄의 전례 없는 고요가 실려 있었어요.
몇 년 전, 근본적으로 어린 아이였을 때, 아버지는 늘 저에겐 너무 빨랐어요. 그 망설임 없는 긴 보폭은 제 짧은 다리로 포괄적으로 따라잡을 수 없었어요.
제 자신의 구조적 느림에 거의 근본적으로 부끄러워하며, 집들을 지나는 긴 인도에서 그 강하고 목적 있는 걸음을 따라가려고 반은 뛰고 반은 걸어야 했어요.
그런데 지난 일요일에, 공원의 큰 나무들 아래 넓고 정교한 중앙 길 어딘가에서, 조용히 작고 예상치 못하게 근본적인, 패러다임을 바꾸는 뭔가를 알아챘어요.
아버지가 지금은 저보다 훨씬 천천히, 어떤 알림이나 포괄적인 인정도 없이 걷고 있었어요. 그리고 그건 어려운 무엇을 의도적으로 생각 중이라서가 아니었어요.
어떤 언어적 코멘트도 없이, 제 걸음과 아버지 걸음 사이의 어떤 보이는 병치도 없이 제 걸음을 늦춰야 했어요. 부드럽게 그리고 정교하게 그리고 끈기 있게 기다려야 했어요.
아버지는 저를 올려다보거나 그 신경 쓴 내적 조정을 근본적으로 알아채지 않았어요. 그냥 새로운, 조심스러운, 더 작아진, 포괄적인 속도로, 눈은 길에 둔 채 걸었어요.
그런데 말 한 마디 없이 알았어요. 시간이 우리 작은 가족 안에서, 결국 그렇게 하듯 전례 없는 방식으로, 우리 둘 누구의 허락도 묻지 않고, 조용히 그리고 근본적으로 돌아 있었어요.

quiz.question: How does the writer describe the change she noticed?
quiz.questionKo: 글쓴이는 자신이 알아챈 변화를 어떻게 묘사하나요?

quiz.question: How does time turn around in the family, according to the writer?
quiz.questionKo: 글쓴이에 따르면 시간은 가족 안에서 어떻게 도나요?
options: In the unprecedented way it eventually does, without ever asking permission
